page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4d server family. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture and is equipped with 378.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 7.88 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include integrated GPUs or local storage, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention from neighboring tenants. Because there is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware, the instance is optimized for workloads that rely on network-attached storage and CPU-bound execution. This hardware profile offers a high memory-to-vCPU density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for memory-intensive applications that do not require specialized hardware accelerators.

Given the substantial memory capacity of 378.0 GB alongside 48 dedicated vCPUs, this server is suited for memory-intensive workloads. Appropriate use cases include medium-to-large database deployments, in-memory caching layers, enterprise application servers, and data processing pipelines that require significant memory footprints without the need for local SSD storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-48 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the c4d server family, configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 378.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of approximately 7.88 GB per core, positioning it as a memory-optimized resource. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource allocation makes the server highly suitable for workloads that demand significant memory capacity and dedicated CPU performance without requiring local disk space. It is suited for database hosting, in-memory analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that utilize network-attached storage. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention from neighboring tenants, making it a reliable option for enterprise workloads.
